problems/_template/CMakeLists.txt.in's gtest_discover_tests lacked TEST_PREFIX, so the skill's documented `ctest --preset debug -R <name>` never matched any test. Fixed by adding TEST_PREFIX "<name>." to the template's gtest_discover_tests call.

tools/rt_symbol_scan.sh used a plain substring grep for _Znwm/_Znam which also matched the harmless placement-new mangled forms (_ZnwmPv/_ZnamPv, used internally by std::optional/Eigen). Fixed by switching to nm -C demangled output with end-anchored patterns (operator new(unsigned long)$) so only the allocating form is flagged.

core_rt_compile_check scanned clean under the release preset but showed a dead (unreachable, never-called) reference to operator new(unsigned long) under debug -O0 -- likely an unoptimized fallback path inside templated stdlib/Eigen code. Confirms /rt-check's choice to gate on the release preset, not debug, is correct and necessary.
Switched CMakePresets.json compiler from g++ to clang++ (18.1.3) after the user installed clang/clang-format/clang-tidy. Full clean rebuild + retest on both presets confirmed green (36/36 tests).

Under clang (unlike gcc), even the release preset shows a static reference to operator new(unsigned long) in core_rt_compile_check, traced via objdump/relocations to Eigen's generic triangular_solve_matrix/gemm_pack_rhs internals (used by .ldlt().solve()) -- present regardless of matrix size, with no guarantee the branch is ever taken for small fixed types. Confirmed via nm -C + objdump -r that no false call site exists for our concrete usage. Redesigned tools/rt_symbol_scan.sh: exceptions/RTTI stay hard failures, allocating operator-new/new[] becomes an advisory pointing at a runtime malloc-guard test. Added RealtimeGuard.LqrDoesNotAllocate and RealtimeGuard.KalmanDoesNotAllocate (Eigen::internal::set_is_malloc_allowed(false) around dlqr/KalmanFilter::update) which both pass, proving no runtime allocation for our fixed 2x2/2x1/1x1 sizes. Documented the distinction in CLAUDE.md and the /rt-check skill.

Implemented the actual cart-pole physics + LQR controller (previously just a linking stub). Derived the linearized point-mass-pendulum-on-cart dynamics via Lagrangian mechanics, discretized via forward Euler, stabilized with harness::control::dlqr. First test attempt used a 5s/250-step convergence window with a 1e-3 norm threshold and failed (actual norm ~4.3e-3) -- cart position/velocity are lightly weighted in Q so they settle much slower than the angle. Fixed by extending to 500 steps (10s), confirmed via a standalone check that this clears the threshold with margin (norm ~2.5e-5 at 500 steps). Lesson: when picking a convergence-test window/tolerance for an LQR-stabilized system, check actual settling time first rather than guessing round numbers.
ninja: build stopped: subcommand failed.

cart-pole linearization had both theta-coupling signs flipped (encoded a hanging pendulum; B matrix was upright-convention, so A and B were mutually inconsistent) and the LQR smoke test passed vacuously because it simulated the closed loop on the same wrong A. Lesson: a closed-loop-on-own-model test can never catch model sign errors — always also assert the open-loop property that motivates the problem (here: monotonic divergence from upright; spectral radius alone is insufficient since forward Euler makes even the hanging model's poles slightly unstable). Guarded by CartPole.OpenLoopUprightIsUnstable.

Council review 2026-07-03 hardened the evolution loop itself: capture_failure.py now anchors build verbs to command position, skips journal-touching commands, classifies decoded stdout/stderr, excerpts a window around the first failure marker (tail-only excerpts used to cut the actual error away), and catches 'ninja: build stopped'/'subcommand failed' (previously silent false negatives under '| tail'). New guard_journal.py PreToolUse hook enforces the append-only journal; /rt-check discovery now reads build/release/realtime_targets.txt (CMake property) instead of grepping source, which had matched commented-out opt-ins and produced a false compliant verdict.

Vendored 13 vetted third-party skills into .claude/skills (10x robotics-agent-skills Apache-2.0, embedded-systems + cpp-pro MIT, arm-cortex-expert MIT cleaned) with licenses/ + THIRD_PARTY.md attribution; CLAUDE.md now scopes the /evolve-only rule to harness-native skills, vendored ones sync from upstream. Ecosystem gap noted: no public control-theory/Kalman/DSP skill exists as of 2026-07.

Added user-directed static-analysis + sanitizer enforcement hooks: lint_cpp.py (PostToolUse Edit|Write|MultiEdit) clang-tidies edited C++ files — headers linted through an including TU (core headers via rt_compile_check.cpp, problem headers via a same-problem compile-DB entry), findings filtered to the edited file, exit 2 feedback, compact static_analysis journal entries (relative paths only). gate_stop.py (Stop) builds + runs the ASan/UBSan debug suite when C++/CMake files changed, blocks stop on red, downgrades to advisory when stop_hook_active to prevent loops, stamps green runs by content digest (no-op 0.04s, green run ~15s, stamp skip 0.04s). Verified via deliberate self-reverted injections (modernize-use-nullptr in cart_pole_model.hpp, static_assert(false) in cart-pole main.cpp) — the static_analysis/build_failure entries of 2026-07-09 ~10:33-10:36 are that verification noise.
